title: hieracium ucenicum
side_effects: Hieracium ucenicum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Ingestion of the plant may lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while excessive exposure can result in headaches and dizziness. Long-term misuse of Hieracium ucenicum may also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as interfere with blood clotting and increase the risk of bleeding. If you are considering using Hieracium ucenicum for health purposes, it is essential to consult with a medical professional to avoid potential risks and side effects.
